description_long: This house features a rectangular footprint with a steeply pitched, gable-style roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The exterior walls are divided into two main color sections: a pale gray and a muted yellow, separated vertically, suggesting a duplex or semi-detached design. Decorative cornices and window trims add classical detailing to the facade, while the windows are symmetrically arranged with simple rectangular frames. Two prominent chimneys, one brick and one metallic, rise from the roof, indicating multiple fireplaces or heating systems. The house sits on a raised foundation with steps leading up to the main entrances, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century European residential architecture.
description: A two-story, semi-detached house with a steep gable roof, dual chimneys, and classical window trims, split into gray and yellow exterior sections.
description_view_0: Rear corner view showing the steep gable roof, two chimneys, and the split color scheme on the side wall.
description_view_1: Side elevation highlighting the long facade, symmetrical window arrangement, and the clear division between yellow and gray wall sections.
description_view_2: Opposite rear corner view emphasizing the yellow section, roof overhang, and visible steps at the base.
description_view_3: Front elevation with two main entry steps, symmetrical window placement, and the split facade colors.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryways with steps, simple doors, and minimal decorative elements
